title: Building Great Agent Skills: The Missing Manual
summary: Matt Pocock presents the Skill Checklist Framework for building high-quality AI agent skills, addressing "skill hell" through structured guidance on triggering (user vs model-invoked), organization (steps and references), steering (leading words for agent guidance), and pruning (removing legacy material and no-ops). The framework emphasizes maintainability, clarity, and intentional agent behavior through minimal skill.md files and context pointers.
writeup: This AI Engineer talk presents the Skill Checklist Framework, a structured method for building and maintaining high-quality agent skills. The framework organizes skill design around four dimensions: Trigger (whether a skill is user-invoked or model-invoked), Structure (its steps and references), Steering (leading words that guide agent reasoning), and Pruning (removing obsolete or ineffective instructions). The stated goal is to give developers a shared rubric for effective, maintainable skills and to avoid the disorganized state described as "skill hell."
